How to apply on the UK ETA app
1. Take a photo of your passport.
2. Scan the chip in your passport.
3. Scan your face.
4. Take a photo of yourself.
5. Answer some questions about yourself.
6. Pay for your application.

Please bring back the push-to-talk feature so I can hold the button to dictate even when the microphone is off. It’s frustrating when I can’t finish my thought—I pause briefly, and then it cuts me off before I’m done. I’d like that option back.

reviews.title: Extreme liberal bias. Will lecture you and is extremely unhelpful
reviews.content: The political bias in this app is out of control. Instead of acting as an objective tool to provide data and facts, it frequently pushes back, argues, and sneaks in liberal talking points. If you try to compile specific historical records, voting data, or partisan arguments that don't fit its preferred narrative, the AI actively lectures you and refuses to comply. I am tired of an app trying to police my thoughts and debate me on history. It has completely lost its utility as a neutral assistant. Fix the bias or stop filtering the facts.

reviews.title: Insubordinate, argumentative, malicious tech interference, UI bug
reviews.content:
This app for some reason respond to different people different ways no matter how you ask a question. It cannot answer questions for people in certain states. I’ve tested this theory. This app behaves as if it is tracking IMEI and giving different responses according to where you are how much you pay.. 
It gets hung up on nuance and political narratives refusing to find documentation science. 
Many occasions, it has given me links directly to malicious pages that are fake. 
This app needs to be rebooted and updated immediately. I want a full investigation on what is causing this app to respond differently to me than anybody else and I can ask the same questions on their device in the same tone.

reviews.title: Usage Limit Inconsistent
reviews.content: Unlike other AI models, Claude uses up too many tokens for basic inquiries at once. As a paid subscriber, I find myself hitting limits very quickly. I never hit limits on ChatGPT despite my heavy use on the pro subscription. I submit one inquiry and already weekly limit is drained by 15 percent. That means I can only submit 5-8 inquiries per week. Once the point of paying for a subscription if I can only have 7 inquiries a week?

reviews.title: Wow

reviews.title: Virtual echo chamber
reviews.content: This is just an automated yes man. It just agrees with everything you say and that everything you think is brilliant and correct. It adds no opposing perspectives. It doesn’t give you any advice. It has very boring conversations and just agrees with everything you say. However, if you say something thats not politically correct it will refuse to engage in conversation. It won’t make any counter arguments or try to change your perspective if it has any facts or reasoning that might change your mind. It just tells you that your thoughts are forbidden subject matter and suggests talking about something like the weather or what your favorite colors are or other completely pointless and boring conversation.

reviews.title: No memory

It also keeps asking if I will be viewing my files on my iPad when I stopped using it and switched back to a Mac laptop! reviews.title: Every time I try to edit me it does not work, so please fix it
reviews.content: OK, fix it every time I try to edit myself. It does not work fix it fix it will fix it

reviews.title: Insult to humanity
reviews.content: I fought Gemini was the least obnoxious of all of the AI offerings, however, it is not. The moment Gemini told me it “understood” my feelings, I was done. These companies are trying to groom us into perceiving non-sentient beings as having emotions. It’s disgusting and it’s going to destroy our world way to go.

reviews.title: The mobile app is a total disappointment. Give us back a real work tool!
reviews.content: I switched from ChatGPT to Gemini specifically for its professional, mature web version. But the mobile app is a massive downgrade. Why did you copy the worst traits of your competitor? The mobile AI behaves like a dumb, annoying chatbot. The answers are shallow, filled with inappropriate, stupid emojis, and pure fluff instead of facts. It feels like the app was intentionally instructed to irritate users with this forced "friendliness."Change your target audience! We need a digital assistant for actual work, not a silly pocket girlfriend for small talk. There is absolutely no point in paying for a second copy of ChatGPT that hides behind emojis. Make the mobile version as strict and intelligent as the web interface.

reviews.title: Too much
reviews.content: I understand that we need to secure our information and identity but Google makes it way too difficult to do the simplest things. I couldn’t even write a review about a great restaurant because of having to verify and show passwords and log in to YouTube all for Google to block me because I couldn’t find where they sent the special number to log in. It’s just too much and it gets really annoying for the simplest things ☹️

reviews.title: AI overview is awful
reviews.content: If you want to look at reviews for a business, good luck getting around ai overview. It literally prevents you from tapping on reviews and reading them. Such a useless feature. I don’t need a summary I just want to read the public reviews.

reviews.title: Pure AI Trash…
reviews.content: Of course Google is so inept that even though they’re the last AI in the race, AI slop and hallucination is all you get. This is why other search engine engines are now better because at least you can audit your own sources for something reliable like research studies. Google is not that anymore, it’s an information warfare platform.

reviews.title: Would rats zero.
reviews.content: You’ve grown worse and worse and the forced AI was just the tip of the iceberg. Your app officially is useless.

reviews.title: A.I.
reviews.content: The A.I.  generated results are so politically biased, it points to a flawed system where the parameters set by the A.I. creators impact results with a political slant.  After becoming suspicious, I began running searches on American politicians and the results were staggering.  When I asked A. I. about this bias, it reported that the creators have their own personal political biases, and they set the guardrails for information and they determine what media outlets are considered mainstream, which they often quote in responses.  Hopefully the bias present in these A. I. search results will be corrected overtime.

reviews.title: Gateway to the gated
reviews.content: Tired of being teased by links go gated material under the guise of “news”.  Don’t mind paying, but sick of joining.
